behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Integration. Runner-up: Unit. Reject Unit because the failure boundary is the data handoff between summary-writing and persisted findings: behavior explicitly says "stored dimension finding" and "for the same round," which requires wiring summary write logic to findings retrieval for that round. Higher levels rejected: no behavior text requiring real OS/network/subprocess or model-quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real local persistence layer (or transactional test DB): seed one round with at least one FAIL dimension finding, attempt to store a PASS summary for that same round, assert write is rejected and no PASS summary is persisted.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Integration. Runner-up: Unit. Reject Unit because the bug is specifically cross-module trust of one store vs another: "reads per-dimension stored findings directly" and "does not rely only on hand-written summary text." This requires wiring gate computation to persistence/repository behavior, not only pure function logic. Higher levels rejected: no explicit need for real OS/process/network or AI output content.
behaviors.test_description: Persist conflicting data for one round (summary text says PASS, stored dimension includes FAIL), execute stop-gate computation through service + repository modules, assert verdict is not PASS and is derived from stored per-dimension findings.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Integration. Runner-up: Unit. Reject Unit because "produce an auditable warning record" implies side-effect persistence/logging plus policy enforcement with the override flag, i.e., module interaction. Higher levels rejected: no text requiring real subprocess/OS/network or real model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise the summary-save path twice: (1) manual override text without override flag -> rejected; (2) with override flag -> accepted and an auditable warning record persisted with actor/time/reason fields.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Integration. Runner-up: System. Reject System because behavior text does not require real git subprocess/OS behavior; it specifies ordering/association logic: "review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit." This can be caught by module wiring over stored commit/round metadata. Higher AI levels rejected: no dependence on model output quality or multi-agent sequence correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Seed commit and review-round metadata where latest fix commit is newer than last review round; attempt PASS finalization and assert rejection. Then seed a newer review round id tied to post-fix commit and assert PASS can proceed.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Agentic. Runner-up: Workflow. Reject Workflow because behavior requires one fresh agent round gate condition, not an explicitly required chain of multiple agentic decisions: "a fresh review-agent round" (singular). Integration rejected by integration-brake(d): this is orchestration/state handoff in an LLM-involved stage (updated diff -> review-agent run -> PASS eligibility), where a mock can hide real handoff failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the live loop with a real review-agent invocation on an updated diff. Verify PASS finalization is blocked until the post-update agent round completes and is associated to that diff; verify attempts without that fresh round are denied.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Integration. Runner-up: Unit. Reject Unit because the behavior depends on persisted cross-round state and policy evaluation over "stored results" + "rounds are exhausted," which is a module/state boundary. Higher levels rejected: no explicit requirement for live model calls; decision is based on stored MUST-FIX findings.
behaviors.test_description: Populate stored results across rounds to exhaustion with at least one unresolved MUST-FIX finding, run gate outcome computation, assert escalation state is emitted and PASS is impossible.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Workflow. Runner-up: Agentic. Reject Agentic because the behavior explicitly requires a multi-step sequence: "live multi-round review workflow" and "exhausted rounds" before enforcing block/escalation. This is not a single model-step check; it validates end-to-end sequencing across rounds. Lower levels rejected by integration-brake(c)/(d): correctness depends on cross-stage handoff over multiple LLM-involved rounds.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end live workflow with multiple review rounds on evolving diffs; force unresolved MUST-FIX findings to persist through configured round limit; assert final state blocks PASS and emits escalation artifact/event after round exhaustion.
